inline_comments.message:
We need to sanitize the input strings here. For example imagine if the xpath being subscribed to is /mynode[@myparam='myvalue']
Now IS_ONGOING_CM_SUBSCRIPTION_CPS_PATH_QUERY becomes

    /datastores/.../filters/filter[@xpath='/mynode[@myparam='myvalue']']

This will lead to a CpsPathException.

Instead we need to replace each single quote ' by doubling them '' so it becomes this:

    /datastores/.../filters/filter[@xpath='/mynode[@myparam=''myvalue'']']

This will parse as intended. From CPS Path Query documentation:

> Quotations marks and apostrophes can be escaped by doubling them in their respective quotes, for example 'CPS ''Path'' Query' -> CPS 'Path' Query

Basically we need a helper method:

    private static String escapeQuotesByDoublingThem(final String value) {
        return value.replace("'", "''");
    }

Could add a leaf condition here to see if it still works

    /cps/path/child[@param='value']

Regarding xpath parameter, we may also want to all normalize it:

    CpsPathUtil.getNormalizedXpath(xpath)

Because the same xpath can be represented multiple ways, e.g. 

    /cps/path[@param='value']
    /cps/path[@param="value"]
    /cps/path[@param = 'value']
etc.
We would see those as different subscriptions unless we normalize. It's a bit of an edge case...
NB: I think we can use cpsDataService.getDataNodes here for a small performance improvement, since we're not using any Cps Path Query -specific features

Both these check are wrong, and need to use .isEmpty()

1) queryDataNodes will return empty collection for no match, never null

2) DataNode.leaves map is initialized to Collections.emptyMap() by default. This can never be null either. (More generally, whether a subscriber list CAN be empty will depend on our later implementation of removing subscriptions.)

I'm not really happy with this test, I think it needs some redesign.

For one thing, given a cpsPathQuery:

    /datastores/datastore[@name='ncmp-datastore:passthrough-running']
     /cm-handles/cm-handle[@id='ch-1']/filters/filter[@xpath='/']

The query can only return either 0 or 1 nodes exactly, not 2. Also, the xpath param of the mock datanode is ('/cps/path') which does not match the one requested ('/')

Ideally we should have at least two tests:

1) Happy path test. Call:

    objectUnderTest.isOngoingCmSubscription(DatastoreType.PASSTHROUGH_RUNNING, 
                                            'ch-1', '/cps/path')

with this mock queryDataNodes response:

    [new DataNode(xpath: '/cps/path', leaves: ['subscribers': 'sub-1'])]

assert response == true

2) No matching node:

    objectUnderTest.isOngoingCmSubscription(DatastoreType.PASSTHROUGH_RUNNING, 
                                            'ch-1', '/non-existing/cps/path')

with mock response:

    []

assert response == false
